No Poverty.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence links the negotiation course directly to poverty eradication or economic resources for the poor.

Zero Hunger.reason: There is no evidence in the course syllabus that relates to ending hunger or improving food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: There is no evidence in the course syllabus that relates to health, well-being, or medical services.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education (Target 4.3) and explicitly cultivates negotiation skills for employment and entrepreneurship (Target 4.4). Core competency B also mentions sustainability considerations (Target 4.7).

Gender Equality.reason: The general mention of 'social responsibility' is too broad to establish a direct link to gender equality targets.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: There is no evidence in the course syllabus that relates to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: There is no evidence in the course syllabus that relates to energy access or renewable energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly aims to cultivate negotiation skills and entrepreneurial approaches, directly supporting productive employment and entrepreneurship (Targets 8.3, 8.5).
evidence: innovative, entrepreneurial approaches to manage change

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Core competencies C and D explicitly mention 'innovative approaches' and 'problem-solving,' fostering innovation within business contexts (Target 9.5).
evidence: discover problems, solve problems & make decisions

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The 'Culture and Psychology' module can foster understanding for inclusive negotiations, a weak link to promoting social inclusion (Target 10.2).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: There is no evidence in the course syllabus that relates to urban development or community sustainability.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core competency B explicitly mentions 'sustainability considerations of business situations,' which is foundational for adopting sustainable practices (Target 12.6).

Climate Action.reason: There is no evidence in the course syllabus that relates to climate change mitigation or adaptation.

Life Below Water.reason: There is no evidence in the course syllabus that relates to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: There is no evidence in the course syllabus that relates to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Negotiation is fundamental for conflict resolution, promoting the rule of law, and building effective, accountable, and transparent institutions (Targets 16.1, 16.3, 16.6, 16.7).
evidence: explore the science and arts of bargaining and negotiation
孫子兵法 (Sun Tzu’s Art of War)

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Negotiation and teamwork are central to the course, directly enabling the formation and strengthening of multi-stakeholder partnerships (Targets 17.16, 17.17).
